title: Stop writing code. Start prompting
post:
I'm going to say something that's going to make senior developers uncomfortable: the most valuable skill for a developer in 2025 isn't writing code. It's knowing how to tell the computer what you want.

I've been coding for 15 years. I've watched frameworks come and go, languages rise and fall. What I'm seeing now is different. AI can write production-quality code in seconds. What it can't do — what no AI can do — is understand what problem you're actually trying to solve.

The developers who are thriving aren't the ones who can code fastest. They're the ones who can think through problems clearly enough to prompt an AI to build the right solution. That's the scarce skill now.

Are you investing in your prompting abilities as much as your coding abilities? Thoughts?
